question: Can my bank charge a prepayment penalty on my floating rate home loan?
expected_answer: No, banks cannot charge foreclosure charges or pre-payment penalties on floating rate home loans to individual borrowers as per RBI guidelines.

question: What is the maximum LTV ratio for a home loan of Rs. 50 lakhs?
expected_answer: For loans above Rs. 30 lakhs and up to Rs. 75 lakhs, the LTV ratio shall not exceed 80%.

question: What is the LTV ratio for a home loan of Rs. 25 lakhs?
expected_answer: For loans up to Rs. 30 lakhs, the LTV ratio shall not exceed 90%.

question: What is the foreclosure charge for a fixed rate home loan?
expected_answer: For fixed rate loans, banks may levy a foreclosure charge not exceeding 2% of the outstanding principal at the time of foreclosure.

question: Within how many days must a bank resolve a customer complaint?
expected_answer: Customer complaints must be acknowledged within 3 working days and resolved within 30 days.

question: What are the priority sector lending targets for domestic banks?
expected_answer: Domestic commercial banks must maintain 40% of ANBC as priority sector lending, with sub-targets of Agriculture: 18%, Micro Enterprises: 7.5%, Weaker Sections: 12%.

question: How many days notice must I give to prepay my home loan?
expected_answer: The borrower must give 7 days written notice to the Lender specifying the amount to prepay.

question: What happens if the bank does not release title documents after full repayment?
expected_answer: The Lender shall release the title documents within 15 working days of full repayment.

question: Within how many days must a bank register mortgage charge with CERSAI?
expected_answer: The Lender shall register the mortgage charge with CERSAI within 30 days.

question: What is the maximum processing fee a bank can charge on a retail loan?
expected_answer: Processing fees shall not exceed 1% of the loan amount for retail loans.

question: What is the maximum waiting period for pre-existing diseases in health insurance?
expected_answer: The maximum waiting period for pre-existing diseases shall not exceed 36 months from the date of first policy inception.

question: What is the initial waiting period in a health insurance policy?
expected_answer: All health insurance policies must have an initial waiting period of 30 days from the date of commencement.

question: How many days before renewal must I apply for health insurance portability?
expected_answer: Portability request must be made at least 45 days before the renewal date.

question: Within how many hours must cashless health insurance claims be settled?
expected_answer: Insurers must settle cashless claims within 3 hours of receiving the final request from the hospital.

question: What is the free look period for a term insurance policy?
expected_answer: The policyholder has a free look period of 30 days from the date of receipt of the policy document.

question: What documents are needed to claim term insurance death benefit?
expected_answer: Required documents include: original policy document, death certificate, claimant's statement, medical attendant's certificate for natural death, and post mortem report and FIR for accidental death.

question: Can an insurer reject a claim citing non-disclosure of pre-existing disease after 8 years?
expected_answer: No, insurers cannot reject claims on grounds of non-disclosure of PED after 8 years of continuous policy.

question: What is the suicide clause in term insurance?
expected_answer: If the life assured commits suicide within 12 months, the insurer pays 80% of total premiums paid. After 12 months, the full sum assured is payable.

question: What is the grace period for monthly premium payment in term insurance?
expected_answer: A grace period of 15 days is allowed for monthly premium payment mode.

question: Within how many days must a lapsed term insurance policy be revived?
expected_answer: A lapsed policy can be revived within 5 years from the due date of the first unpaid premium.

question: What is the minimum equity allocation for an equity mutual fund?
expected_answer: Equity mutual funds must invest a minimum of 65% of total assets in equity and equity-related instruments.

question: What is the maximum TER for an equity mutual fund with AUM up to Rs. 500 crore?
expected_answer: The maximum Total Expense Ratio (TER) for equity-oriented schemes with AUM up to Rs. 500 crore is 2.25%.

question: Within how many days must AMCs publish monthly portfolio?
expected_answer: AMCs shall publish the portfolio of each scheme on their website within 10 days from the close of each month.

question: How many days notice must a mutual fund give before changing fundamental attributes?
expected_answer: AMCs must disclose any material changes to the scheme's fundamental attributes with 30 days' notice to unitholders.

question: Within how many business days are equity mutual fund redemptions credited?
expected_answer: Redemption proceeds must be credited within 3 business days for equity schemes.

question: What is the maximum UPI transaction limit for regular payments?
expected_answer: The maximum amount per UPI transaction is Rs. 1,00,000 (one lakh).

question: What is the UPI transaction limit for insurance payments?
expected_answer: For insurance services, the UPI transaction limit is Rs. 2,00,000 (two lakhs).

question: Can a merchant charge MDR for UPI payments?
expected_answer: No MDR shall be charged on UPI transactions. Any merchant found charging customers for UPI payments shall be reported to the acquiring bank.

question: Within how many days must I report an unauthorised UPI transaction to limit liability?
expected_answer: Customers must report unauthorised transactions to their bank within 3 working days to avail zero or limited liability benefits.

question: What is the timeline for final resolution of a UPI dispute?
expected_answer: The final resolution must be provided within 90 days of reporting the unauthorised transaction.

question: What is the periodic review frequency for high-risk KYC accounts?
expected_answer: High-risk accounts must undergo periodic review every 2 years.

question: How long must banks retain KYC records after account closure?
expected_answer: Records shall be maintained for five years from the date of cessation of the business relationship, or ten years from the date of the transaction, whichever is later.

question: What documents are acceptable for KYC identity verification?
expected_answer: Acceptable KYC documents include Aadhaar card, PAN card, passport, voter ID, and driving licence.

question: Are Politically Exposed Persons classified as high-risk for KYC?
expected_answer: Yes, Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) are automatically classified as high-risk and must undergo Enhanced Due Diligence.

question: What is the maximum review period for low-risk KYC customers?
expected_answer: Low-risk accounts need periodic review every 10 years.

question: क्या बैंक मेरे फ्लोटिंग रेट होम लोन पर प्री-पेमेंट चार्ज लगा सकता है?
expected_answer: नहीं, RBI के दिशानिर्देशों के अनुसार बैंक फ्लोटिंग रेट होम लोन पर प्री-पेमेंट पेनल्टी नहीं लगा सकते।

question: स्वास्थ्य बीमा में पोर्टेबिलिटी के लिए कितने दिन पहले आवेदन करना होगा?
expected_answer: पोर्टेबिलिटी के लिए नवीनीकरण की तारीख से कम से कम 45 दिन पहले आवेदन करना होगा।

question: UPI पर प्रति लेनदेन अधिकतम सीमा क्या है?
expected_answer: UPI पर प्रति लेनदेन अधिकतम राशि 1,00,000 रुपये है।

question: टर्म इंश्योरेंस पॉलिसी का फ्री लुक पीरियड कितने दिनों का होता है?
expected_answer: पॉलिसी दस्तावेज प्राप्त होने की तारीख से 30 दिन का फ्री लुक पीरियड होता है।

question: म्यूचुअल फंड में न्यूनतम इक्विटी निवेश कितना होना चाहिए?
expected_answer: इक्विटी म्यूचुअल फंड को अपनी कुल संपत्ति का न्यूनतम 65% इक्विटी में निवेश करना होता है।

question: What percentage of AUM must a small-cap fund allocate to small-cap stocks?
expected_answer: Small-cap funds must invest in stocks ranked 251st onwards. The multi-cap category requires minimum 25% each in large, mid, and small cap.

question: What is the UPI transaction limit for tax payments?
expected_answer: For tax payments, the UPI transaction limit is Rs. 5,00,000 (five lakhs).

question: What is the interest rate charged if reimbursement claims are delayed beyond 30 days?
expected_answer: Interest at 2% above bank rate is payable if claims are delayed beyond 30 days.

question: Within how many days must the new insurer process health insurance portability?
expected_answer: The new insurer must process the portability within 15 days. If the new insurer does not respond within this period, it is deemed to have accepted the portability.

question: What is Biometric-based eKYC used for?
expected_answer: Biometric-based e-KYC allows full-service accounts, while OTP-based e-KYC is permitted for accounts with transaction limits.
